title: fix(#1789): unblock Code Review Quality dashboard pipeline
body:
Fixes #1789.

## Summary

The Code Review Quality dashboard at `/analytics/code-review-quality` shows
"No code review data yet" in prod despite the publisher logging hundreds of
"Published … event" messages. Investigation found four independent bugs
along the publish → Pub/Sub → BQ-sink → dashboard-query path; each
independently zeros out a panel, and together they trigger the empty-state
guard.

This PR closes all four plus three related issues uncovered while
validating the fixes end-to-end.

## What changed

| # | File | Bug |
|---|------|-----|
| 1 | `Dockerfile` | Copy `schemas/` into the prod image so `code-review-publisher.ts` can load `finding.schema.json` at runtime (was failing with `ENOENT`, killing every PR event) |
| 2 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | Add `event_type` attribute to `publishFinding` / `publishReviewCompleted`. The BQ-sink subscription filters on `attributes.event_type`; without it Pub/Sub silently dropped every finding/review message |
| 3 | `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts` | Findings + runs queries now reference `aictrl_telemetry` (where the BQ subscriptions actually write) instead of the non-existent `aictrl_code_review.code_review_findings` / `code_review_runs` |
| 4 | `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts` | Add `created_at` partition predicate to the noisy-rules query (table sets `require_partition_filter = true`) |
| 5 | `server/services/code-review-publisher.ts` | `buildPrEventPayload` now emits field names + nested structs matching the `code_review_pull_requests` BQ schema (`id`, `number`, `repository_full_name` + `repository` struct, `author` struct, `stats` struct). Old flat names (`pr_id`, `pr_number`, `repo_*`, `author_*`, `stats_*`) would have dead-lettered every insert under `drop_unknown_fields` once bug 1 stopped blocking publish |
| 6 | `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts` | PR funnel uses `stats.time_to_first_review_ms IS NOT NULL` instead of the non-existent top-level `first_review_at` column |
| 7 | `schemas/code-review/v1/finding.schema.json` | Add `hasFindingsMin` and `latestReviewId` to the `PullRequest` def. They're real fields on the runtime `PullRequest` type and would otherwise fail `additionalProperties:false` validation as soon as bug 1 stopped blocking schema load |

Adds `test/code-review-publisher.test.ts` (12 tests) as a regression lock
for the `event_type` attribute and the PR payload schema.

## Out of scope

The `code_review_findings` and `code_review_runs` tables have their own
payload-shape divergence from `publishFinding` / `publishReviewCompleted`
output (missing required `sha`, `bot`, `rule_slug`, `ingestion_source`,
plus mis-named `start_line` / `title` / `suggestion`). Once this PR
deploys, the dashboard's PR-funnel section will populate; findings,
duration, and noisy-rules cards will continue to show their own per-card
empty states until that follow-up PR. (Will open a separate issue.)

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run test/code-review-publisher.test.ts test/mcp/code-review.test.ts test/api/github-app-routes.test.ts test/code-review/` — 150 tests pass
- [x] `npm run lint` — clean
- [x] `npx ast-grep scan` — only pre-existing warning in `step-matcher.ts:24` (unrelated)
- [x] `npm run check:types-mirror` — 10 types OK
- [x] `npm run check:knip` — clean
- [x] `npm run build` — TypeScript compile passes
- [ ] After deploy to sandbox: PR funnel populates with PRs from the last 30 days; dashboard renders charts instead of the empty state

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
diff:
diff --git a/Dockerfile b/Dockerfile
index 325f9919e..2b1e8a7a2 100644
--- a/Dockerfile
+++ b/Dockerfile
@@ -70,6 +70,11 @@ COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
 # Copy non-TS assets that compiled code references
 COPY server/api/install-script.sh ./dist/server/api/install-script.sh
 
+# Copy JSON schemas referenced by code-review-publisher.ts via path resolution
+# from import.meta.url (../../schemas/...). Without this the publisher fails
+# to load finding.schema.json at runtime and silently drops every event.
+COPY schemas ./dist/schemas
+
 # Copy built UI to serve statically
 COPY --from=builder /app/ui/dist ./ui/dist
 
diff --git a/schemas/code-review/v1/finding.schema.json b/schemas/code-review/v1/finding.schema.json
index 8f32021d8..7d1864ec2 100644
--- a/schemas/code-review/v1/finding.schema.json
+++ b/schemas/code-review/v1/finding.schema.json
@@ -531,6 +531,15 @@
           "enum": ["webhook", "backfill", "manual"],
           "description": "How this PR record entered the data layer. `webhook` = live event from GitHub App. `backfill` = one-off historical import via gh CLI / GraphQL pagination. `manual` = developer entered for testing."
         },
+        "hasFindingsMin": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "enum": ["critical", "high", "medium", "low", "info", null],
+          "description": "Severity of the most-severe finding ever recorded on this PR (across all reviews). Denormalised from the findings subcollection by completeReview / recordFinding so the list endpoint can filter by `hasFindingsMin` without fanning out. Null until a review is completed."
+        },
+        "latestReviewId": {
+          "type": ["string", "null"],
+          "description": "ID of the most recently completed review for this PR. Denormalised by completeReview so the dashboard can deep-link to it without an extra query."
+        },
         "createdAt": {
           "type": "string",
           "format": "date-time",
diff --git a/server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts b/server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts
index e2b528012..43b0dfcc2 100644
--- a/server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts
+++ b/server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts
@@ -1,10 +1,14 @@
 /**
  * Code Review Quality Analytics API Routes
  *
- * Queries BigQuery tables in `aictrl_code_review` dataset:
- *   - code_review_findings   → finding volume, actionability, noisy rules
- *   - code_review_runs       → review duration p50/p95
- *   - code_review_pull_requests → PR funnel stages
+ * Queries BigQuery tables across two datasets:
+ *   - aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings    → finding volume, actionability, noisy rules
+ *   - aictrl_telemetry.code_review_runs        → review duration p50/p95
+ *   - aictrl_code_review.code_review_pull_requests → PR funnel stages
+ *
+ * (Findings + runs land in the telemetry dataset alongside other event-stream
+ * tables; pull requests live in the dedicated code_review dataset because they
+ * are the operational source-of-truth, not just an event stream.)
  *
  * All queries are org-scoped and require org membership.
  * Results are cached for 5 minutes to reduce BQ slot consumption.
@@ -92,6 +96,12 @@ export interface CodeReviewQualityResponse {
 // BQ queries
 // =============================================================================
 
+// All four findings/runs queries below MUST include a predicate over their
+// partition column (created_at / started_at) — both tables set
+// require_partition_filter = true (terraform/bigquery.tf, terraform/code-review-runs.tf).
+// Querying without one returns "Cannot query over table … without a filter
+// over column(s) '…' that can be used for partition elimination".
+
 // 1. Findings volume by day × severity — last 30 days
 const FINDINGS_BY_DAY_QUERY = `
 SELECT
@@ -102,7 +112,7 @@ SELECT
   COUNTIF(severity = 'low')      AS low,
   COUNTIF(severity = 'info')     AS info,
   COUNT(*) AS total
-FROM \`aictrl_code_review.code_review_findings\`
+FROM \`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings\`
 WHERE org_id = @orgId
   AND created_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
 GROUP BY date
@@ -115,9 +125,10 @@ SELECT
   bot,
   COUNT(*) AS total_findings,
   COUNTIF(actionable = TRUE) AS actionable_findings
-FROM \`aictrl_code_review.code_review_findings\`
+FROM \`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings\`
 WHERE org_id = @orgId
   AND actionable IS NOT NULL
+  AND created_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
 GROUP BY bot
 ORDER BY total_findings DESC
 LIMIT 20
@@ -129,7 +140,7 @@ SELECT
   APPROX_QUANTILES(duration_ms, 100)[OFFSET(50)] AS p50_ms,
   APPROX_QUANTILES(duration_ms, 100)[OFFSET(95)] AS p95_ms,
   COUNT(*) AS sample_size
-FROM \`aictrl_code_review.code_review_runs\`
+FROM \`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_runs\`
 WHERE org_id = @orgId
   AND duration_ms IS NOT NULL
   AND started_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
@@ -141,10 +152,11 @@ SELECT
   rule_slug,
   COUNTIF(actionable = FALSE) AS fp_count,
   COUNT(*) AS total_count
-FROM \`aictrl_code_review.code_review_findings\`
+FROM \`aictrl_telemetry.code_review_findings\`
 WHERE org_id = @orgId
   AND actionable IS NOT NULL
   AND rule_slug IS NOT NULL
+  AND created_at >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
 GROUP BY rule_slug
 HAVING fp_count > 0
 ORDER BY fp_count DESC
@@ -152,10 +164,13 @@ LIMIT 10
 `;
 
 // 5. PR funnel: opened → first review → merged
+// "first review" is derived from stats.time_to_first_review_ms (set by the
+// review pipeline once a review is completed) — the table has no top-level
+// first_review_at column.
 const PR_FUNNEL_QUERY = `
 SELECT
   COUNT(*) AS opened,
-  COUNTIF(first_review_at IS NOT NULL) AS first_review,
+  COUNTIF(stats.time_to_first_review_ms IS NOT NULL) AS first_review,
   COUNTIF(merged_at IS NOT NULL) AS merged
 FROM \`aictrl_code_review.code_review_pull_requests\`
 WHERE org_id = @orgId
diff --git a/server/services/code-review-publisher.ts b/server/services/code-review-publisher.ts
index 9694f5c32..9b74ec9e8 100644
--- a/server/services/code-review-publisher.ts
+++ b/server/services/code-review-publisher.ts
@@ -252,7 +252,11 @@ export async function publishFinding(finding: CodeReviewFinding): Promise<void>
     updated_at: finding.updatedAt ?? null,
   };
 
+  // event_type MUST be present so the Pub/Sub BQ-sink subscription filter
+  // (`attributes.event_type = "code_review.finding_recorded"`) matches.
+  // Without this attribute the message is silently dropped before reaching BQ.
   const attributes: Record<string, string> = {
+    event_type: 'code_review.finding_recorded',
     org_id: finding.orgId,
     finding_id: finding.id,
     review_id: finding.reviewId,
@@ -312,7 +316,11 @@ export async function publishReviewCompleted(review: CodeReview): Promise<void>
     skill_id: review.skillId ?? null,
   };
 
+  // event_type MUST be present so the Pub/Sub BQ-sink subscription filter
+  // (`attributes.event_type = "code_review.review_completed"`) matches.
+  // Without this attribute the message is silently dropped before reaching BQ.
   const attributes: Record<string, string> = {
+    event_type: 'code_review.review_completed',
     org_id: review.orgId,
     review_id: review.id,
     pr_id: review.prId,
@@ -353,68 +361,89 @@ function buildPrEventPayload(
   const eventId = randomUUID();
   const now = new Date().toISOString();
 
+  // Field names + nesting MUST match the BigQuery schema in
+  // terraform/code-review-pull-requests.tf — the BQ subscription has
+  // `use_table_schema = true` + `drop_unknown_fields = true`, so any
+  // mismatch on a REQUIRED column dead-letters the message.
   return {
+    // ── Event metadata (extra fields, dropped by BQ via drop_unknown_fields) ──
     event_id: eventId,
     event_type: eventType,
     event_timestamp: now,
-    schema_version: '1.0.0',
 
-    // PR identity
-    pr_id: pr.id,
+    // ── Identity (REQUIRED columns) ──────────────────────────────────────
+    schema_version: '1.0.0',
+    id: pr.id,
     org_id: pr.orgId,
-    pr_number: pr.number,
+
+    // ── Repository (REQUIRED struct + denormalised cluster key) ──────────
+    repository: {
+      id: pr.repository.id ?? null,
+      full_name: pr.repository.fullName,
+      platform: pr.repository.platform,
+      url: pr.repository.url ?? null,
+      default_branch: pr.repository.defaultBranch ?? null,
+    },
+    repository_full_name: pr.repository.fullName,
+
+    // ── Core PR fields ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    number: pr.number,
     url: pr.url,
     title: pr.title,
     description: pr.description,
     base_branch: pr.baseBranch,
     head_branch: pr.headBranch,
-    state: pr.state,
-    draft: pr.draft,
-
-    // Repository context (denormalised for BigQuery)
-    repo_id: pr.repository.id,
-    repo_full_name: pr.repository.fullName,
-    repo_platform: pr.repository.platform,
-    repo_url: pr.repository.url,
-    repo_default_branch: pr.repository.defaultBranch,
 
-    // Author
-    author_username: pr.author?.username ?? null,
-    author_id: pr.author?.id ?? null,
-    author_type: pr.author?.type ?? null,
+    // ── Author (NULLABLE struct) ──────────────────────────────────────────
+    author: pr.author
+      ? {
+          username: pr.author.username ?? null,
+          id: pr.author.id ?? null,
+          type: pr.author.type ?? null,
+        }
+      : null,
 
-    // Labels / linked issues
+    // ── Labels / linked issues ────────────────────────────────────────────
     labels: pr.labels,
     linked_issues: pr.linkedIssues,
 
-    // Timestamps
+    // ── State & timestamps ────────────────────────────────────────────────
+    state: pr.state,
+    draft: pr.draft,
     opened_at: pr.openedAt,
     last_sync_at: pr.lastSyncAt,
     merged_at: pr.mergedAt,
     closed_at: pr.closedAt,
 
-    // Diff stats
+    // ── SHAs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     current_sha: pr.currentSha,
     merge_sha: pr.mergeSha,
+
+    // ── Diff size ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     additions: pr.additions,
     deletions: pr.deletions,
     changed_files: pr.changedFiles,
     commit_count: pr.commitCount,
 
-    // Aggregate stats snapshot
-    stats_review_count: pr.stats?.reviewCount ?? null,
-    stats_finding_count: pr.stats?.findingCount ?? null,
-    stats_actionable_finding_count: pr.stats?.actionableFindingCount ?? null,
-    stats_actionability_rate: pr.stats?.actionabilityRate ?? null,
-    stats_time_to_first_review_ms: pr.stats?.timeToFirstReviewMs ?? null,
-    stats_time_to_merge_ms: pr.stats?.timeToMergeMs ?? null,
-
-    // Ingestion metadata
+    // ── Stats (NULLABLE struct) ───────────────────────────────────────────
+    stats: pr.stats
+      ? {
+          review_count: pr.stats.reviewCount ?? null,
+          finding_count: pr.stats.findingCount ?? null,
+          actionable_finding_count: pr.stats.actionableFindingCount ?? null,
+          actionability_rate: pr.stats.actionabilityRate ?? null,
+          time_to_first_review_ms: pr.stats.timeToFirstReviewMs ?? null,
+          time_to_merge_ms: pr.stats.timeToMergeMs ?? null,
+        }
+      : null,
+
+    // ── Ingestion metadata ────────────────────────────────────────────────
     ingestion_source: pr.ingestionSource,
-    pr_created_at: pr.createdAt,
-    pr_updated_at: pr.updatedAt,
+    created_at: pr.createdAt,
+    updated_at: pr.updatedAt,
 
-    // Extra caller-provided fields (e.g. webhook_action for pr_opened events)
+    // Extra caller-provided fields (e.g. webhook_action for pr_opened events).
+    // These are not in the BQ schema; drop_unknown_fields silently discards them.
     ...extraFields,
   };
 }
@@ -428,12 +457,16 @@ async function publishPrEvent(
   const attributes: Record<string, string> = {
     org_id: pr.orgId,
     pr_id: pr.id,
-    repo_full_name: pr.repository.fullName,
+    repository_full_name: pr.repository.fullName,
   };
 
   try {
-    // Use validateAndPublish (same as finding/review publishers) so the PR domain
-    // object is validated against $defs.PullRequest before publishing.
+    // Use validateAndPublish so the PR domain object is validated against
+    // $defs.PullRequest before publishing. (Note: publishFinding /
+    // publishReviewCompleted intentionally bypass this — the new
+    // CodeReviewFinding / CodeReview storage types diverged from the
+    // schema's $defs.Finding / $defs.Review. Reconciliation is tracked
+    // out-of-scope to #1789.)
     await validateAndPublish(eventType, pr, 'PullRequest', payload, attributes);
 
     log.info(
diff --git a/test/code-review-publisher.test.ts b/test/code-review-publisher.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..efeacc944
--- /dev/null
+++ b/test/code-review-publisher.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,311 @@
+/**
+ * Unit Tests: code-review-publisher
+ *
+ * Regression tests for the bugs that left the Code Review Quality dashboard
+ * blank in prod (#1789):
+ *
+ *   - publishFinding / publishReviewCompleted MUST set `event_type` in
+ *     Pub/Sub attributes. The BQ-sink subscriptions filter on
+ *     `attributes.event_type = "..."`; missing it silently drops the message.
+ *
+ *   - buildPrEventPayload MUST emit field names + nesting matching the
+ *     code_review_pull_requests BigQuery schema (id, number,
+ *     repository_full_name, repository struct, author struct, stats struct).
+ */
+
+import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
+import type {
+  CodeReview,
+  CodeReviewFinding,
+  PullRequest,
+} from '../server/state/types.js';
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Mocks (must be set up before any import of the module under test)
+//
+// vi.mock() calls are hoisted above plain `const` declarations at parse time,
+// so any variable referenced inside a vi.mock factory must come from
+// vi.hoisted() — otherwise the factory closes over a TDZ binding.
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+const { mockPublishMessage, mockTopic } = vi.hoisted(() => {
+  const publishMessage = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue('msg-id');
+  return {
+    mockPublishMessage: publishMessage,
+    mockTopic: vi.fn(() => ({ publishMessage })),
+  };
+});
+
+vi.mock('@google-cloud/pubsub', () => ({
+  PubSub: vi.fn(() => ({ topic: mockTopic })),
+}));
+
+const mockLogger = vi.hoisted(() => ({
+  error: vi.fn(),
+  warn: vi.fn(),
+  info: vi.fn(),
+  debug: vi.fn(),
+}));
+
+vi.mock('../server/lib/logger.js', () => ({
+  logger: mockLogger,
+  createLogger: () => mockLogger,
+}));
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Module under test (imported after mocks)
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+import {
+  publishFinding,
+  publishReviewCompleted,
+  publishPrOpened,
+  publishPrSynchronized,
+  publishPrMerged,
+  publishPrClosed,
+} from '../server/services/code-review-publisher.js';
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Fixtures
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+function makeFinding(overrides: Partial<CodeReviewFinding> = {}): CodeReviewFinding {
+  return {
+    id: 'finding-1',
+    reviewId: 'review-1',
+    prId: 'pr-1',
+    orgId: 'org-1',
+    severity: 'high',
+    title: 'Possible null deref',
+    description: 'Foo could be null here',
+    file: 'src/foo.ts',
+    startLine: 10,
+    endLine: 12,
+    suggestion: 'Add null check',
+    createdAt: '2026-05-05T12:00:00.000Z',
+    ...overrides,
+  };
+}
+
+function makeReview(overrides: Partial<CodeReview> = {}): CodeReview {
+  return {
+    id: 'review-1',
+    prId: 'pr-1',
+    orgId: 'org-1',
+    status: 'completed',
+    startedAt: '2026-05-05T12:00:00.000Z',
+    completedAt: '2026-05-05T12:01:30.000Z',
+    findingCount: 3,
+    findingsBySeverity: { critical: 0, high: 1, medium: 1, low: 1, info: 0 },
+    durationMs: 90_000,
+    ...overrides,
+  };
+}
+
+function makePullRequest(overrides: Partial<PullRequest> = {}): PullRequest {
+  return {
+    schemaVersion: '1.0.0',
+    id: 'org-1-aictrl-dev-aictrl-1648',
+    orgId: 'org-1',
+    repository: {
+      id: 'repo-conn-1',
+      fullName: 'aictrl-dev/aictrl',
+      platform: 'github',
+      url: 'https://github.com/aictrl-dev/aictrl',
+      defaultBranch: 'main',
+    },
+    number: 1648,
+    url: 'https://github.com/aictrl-dev/aictrl/pull/1648',
+    title: 'A PR',
+    description: 'PR body',
+    baseBranch: 'sandbox',
+    headBranch: 'feature/x',
+    author: { username: 'octocat', id: '12345', type: 'user' },
+    labels: ['bug'],
+    linkedIssues: ['aictrl-dev/aictrl#1700'],
+    state: 'open',
+    draft: false,
+    openedAt: '2026-05-05T11:00:00.000Z',
+    lastSyncAt: '2026-05-05T11:30:00.000Z',
+    mergedAt: null,
+    closedAt: null,
+    currentSha: 'a'.repeat(40),
+    mergeSha: null,
+    additions: 100,
+    deletions: 20,
+    changedFiles: 5,
+    commitCount: 3,
+    stats: {
+      reviewCount: 1,
+      findingCount: 3,
+      actionableFindingCount: null,
+      actionabilityRate: null,
+      timeToFirstReviewMs: 60_000,
+      timeToMergeMs: null,
+    },
+    hasFindingsMin: 'high',
+    latestReviewId: 'review-1',
+    ingestionSource: 'webhook',
+    createdAt: '2026-05-05T11:00:01.000Z',
+    updatedAt: '2026-05-05T11:30:01.000Z',
+    ...overrides,
+  };
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Tests
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+describe('code-review-publisher — Pub/Sub attribute contract (#1789)', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks();
+    delete process.env.PUBSUB_DISABLED;
+  });
+
+  // The BQ-sink subscriptions filter on `attributes.event_type`. The publisher
+  // bypassed validateAndPublish for findings/reviews and forgot to add the
+  // attribute, so messages were silently dropped before reaching BigQuery.
+  // These tests are the regression lock for that bug.
+
+  describe('publishFinding', () => {
+    it('publishes with event_type=code_review.finding_recorded in attributes', async () => {
+      await publishFinding(makeFinding());
+
+      expect(mockPublishMessage).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
+      const call = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+      expect(call.attributes).toMatchObject({
+        event_type: 'code_review.finding_recorded',
+        org_id: 'org-1',
+        finding_id: 'finding-1',
+        review_id: 'review-1',
+        pr_id: 'pr-1',
+      });
+    });
+
+    it('publishes the expected event_type in the JSON payload as well', async () => {
+      await publishFinding(makeFinding());
+      const call = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+      expect(call.json.event_type).toBe('code_review.finding_recorded');
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('publishReviewCompleted', () => {
+    it('publishes with event_type=code_review.review_completed in attributes', async () => {
+      await publishReviewCompleted(makeReview());
+
+      expect(mockPublishMessage).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
+      const call = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+      expect(call.attributes).toMatchObject({
+        event_type: 'code_review.review_completed',
+        org_id: 'org-1',
+        review_id: 'review-1',
+        pr_id: 'pr-1',
+      });
+    });
+  });
+
+  describe('PR lifecycle publishers', () => {
+    it.each([
+      ['publishPrOpened', publishPrOpened, 'code_review.pr_opened'],
+      ['publishPrSynchronized', publishPrSynchronized, 'code_review.pr_synchronized'],
+      ['publishPrMerged', publishPrMerged, 'code_review.pr_merged'],
+      ['publishPrClosed', publishPrClosed, 'code_review.pr_closed'],
+    ] as const)('%s sets event_type=%s in attributes', async (_name, fn, expected) => {
+      await fn(makePullRequest());
+
+      expect(mockPublishMessage).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
+      const call = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+      expect(call.attributes.event_type).toBe(expected);
+      expect(call.attributes.org_id).toBe('org-1');
+      expect(call.attributes.pr_id).toBe('org-1-aictrl-dev-aictrl-1648');
+      // Routing attribute mirrors the BQ column name to avoid divergence.
+      expect(call.attributes.repository_full_name).toBe('aictrl-dev/aictrl');
+      expect(call.attributes).not.toHaveProperty('repo_full_name');
+    });
+  });
+});
+
+describe('code-review-publisher — PR payload matches BigQuery schema (#1789)', () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.clearAllMocks();
+    delete process.env.PUBSUB_DISABLED;
+  });
+
+  // Field names + struct shape MUST match terraform/code-review-pull-requests.tf.
+  // The BQ subscription has drop_unknown_fields = true, so any rename or
+  // un-nesting that drops a REQUIRED column dead-letters the message.
+
+  it('emits required top-level fields with the correct names', async () => {
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest());
+
+    const { json } = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+
+    // REQUIRED columns from the BQ schema
+    expect(json.schema_version).toBe('1.0.0');
+    expect(json.id).toBe('org-1-aictrl-dev-aictrl-1648');
+    expect(json.org_id).toBe('org-1');
+    expect(json.repository_full_name).toBe('aictrl-dev/aictrl');
+    expect(json.number).toBe(1648);
+    expect(json.url).toBe('https://github.com/aictrl-dev/aictrl/pull/1648');
+    expect(json.state).toBe('open');
+    expect(json.opened_at).toBe('2026-05-05T11:00:00.000Z');
+    expect(json.ingestion_source).toBe('webhook');
+
+    // Old flat names that previously broke BQ inserts must be gone
+    expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('pr_number');
+    expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('pr_id');
+    expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('repo_full_name');
+  });
+
+  it('emits the repository struct with required nested fields', async () => {
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest());
+    const { json } = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+
+    expect(json.repository).toEqual({
+      id: 'repo-conn-1',
+      full_name: 'aictrl-dev/aictrl',
+      platform: 'github',
+      url: 'https://github.com/aictrl-dev/aictrl',
+      default_branch: 'main',
+    });
+  });
+
+  it('emits the author struct (or null when absent)', async () => {
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest());
+    const callWithAuthor = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+    expect(callWithAuthor.json.author).toEqual({
+      username: 'octocat',
+      id: '12345',
+      type: 'user',
+    });
+
+    mockPublishMessage.mockClear();
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest({ author: null }));
+    const callNoAuthor = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+    expect(callNoAuthor.json.author).toBeNull();
+  });
+
+  it('emits the stats struct with snake_case nested fields', async () => {
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest());
+    const { json } = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+
+    expect(json.stats).toEqual({
+      review_count: 1,
+      finding_count: 3,
+      actionable_finding_count: null,
+      actionability_rate: null,
+      time_to_first_review_ms: 60_000,
+      time_to_merge_ms: null,
+    });
+
+    // Old flat names must be gone
+    expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('stats_review_count');
+    expect(json).not.toHaveProperty('stats_time_to_first_review_ms');
+  });
+
+  it('forwards the webhook_action extra field on pr_opened', async () => {
+    await publishPrOpened(makePullRequest(), 'reopened');
+    const { json } = mockPublishMessage.mock.calls[0][0];
+    expect(json.webhook_action).toBe('reopened');
+  });
+});

botReviews.body:
## Code Review: fix(#1789) — unblock Code Review Quality dashboard pipeline

**Files reviewed:** 5 | **Findings:** 1 MINOR | **Subagents:** Security, Consistency, Bug-Hunter

---

| # | File | Line | Severity | Rule | Summary |
|---|------|------|----------|------|---------|
| 1 | `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts` | 99 | MINOR | consistency (comment-accuracy) | Comment says "three" queries but four hit findings/runs tables |

### Details

**1. Comment says "three" queries but four hit findings/runs tables** — `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts:99`

The comment reads _"All three findings/runs queries below MUST include a predicate…"_, yet there are four such queries: `FINDINGS_BY_DAY_QUERY` (findings), `FINDINGS_BY_BOT_QUERY` (findings), `REVIEW_DURATION_QUERY` (runs), and `FALSE_POSITIVE_RULES_QUERY` (findings). A future reader could miscount and assume one query is missing a partition filter.

**Fix:** Change "All three" → "All four".

---

Reviewed SHA: fcb9a15e77f860a1daa9c1562fd5b4a71ab01026

## Code Review — PR #1790

**Bot:** aictrl-dev · **Model:** glm-5.1 · **Concerns:** Security, Consistency, Bug Hunter (1 shard)

---

### Findings: 1

| # | Severity | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|----------|------|------|------|---------|
| 1 | MINOR | `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts` | 96 | docs | Partition-filter comment scope is ambiguous |

---

#### 1. MINOR — `server/api/code-review-analytics-routes.ts:96` — docs / comment-scope

**Partition-filter comment scope is ambiguous**

The block comment at line 96 says "All four findings/runs queries below MUST include a predicate over their partition column," but the file contains five queries total (findings-by-day, bot-actionability, review-durations, noisy-rules, PR-funnel). The count of "four" does not match, and query 5 targets a different dataset (`aictrl_code_review` vs `aictrl_telemetry`).

**Suggested fix:** Rewrite to scope the comment to queries 1–4 only, e.g.: "Queries 1–4 target `aictrl_telemetry` tables with `require_partition_filter = true`; each must include a `created_at` or `started_at` predicate. Query 5 targets the dedicated `aictrl_code_review` dataset (partitioned on `opened_at`)."

---

*No security, blocker, or major findings detected. The PR correctly fixes the four independent bugs that zeroed out the dashboard (missing `event_type` Pub/Sub attributes, wrong BQ dataset references, missing partition predicates, and payload field-name mismatches). The `hasFindingsMin`/`latestReviewId` fields are Firestore-only denormalisations with no corresponding BQ columns, so their absence from `buildPrEventPayload` is intentional — `drop_unknown_fields` would discard them anyway. The PR funnel query correctly filters on `opened_at`, which matches the partition column in `terraform/code-review-pull-requests.tf:123`.*

Reviewed SHA: f10cb165bbb9b03b5c25bc7d221346ed38698cd4
